data.title: Pharvaris: Late-Stage Deucrictibant Progress, Expansion into Untreated Angioedema Markets, and Solid Cash Runway Underpin Buy Rating and $60 Target
data.description:
H.C. Wainwright analyst Patrick Trucchio reiterated a Buy rating on Pharvaris today and set a price target of $60.00.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Patrick Trucchio has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, primarily the advancement of Pharvaris’s late‑stage deucrictibant franchise across multiple hereditary and acquired angioedema settings. He highlights the completion of enrollment in the pivotal CHAPTER‑3 Phase 3 prophylaxis trial, the maintained timeline for a 3Q26 topline readout, and the planned 1H26 NDA submission for the on‑demand capsule formulation supported by positive RAPIDe‑2 and RAPIDe‑3 data.

He also points to progress in the CREAATE Phase 3 study in acquired C1‑inhibitor deficiency angioedema, which he views as a meaningful expansion opportunity into a largely untreated market. Trucchio underscores the company’s strong balance sheet, with year‑end cash of about €292 million expected to fund operations through the CHAPTER‑3 data readout into 1H27, and models substantial peak revenue potential for deucrictibant in prophylaxis, supporting his reiterated Buy rating and $60 price target.

In another report released today, RBC Capital also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $51.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Organto: Accelerating Growth and Strategic Expansion Underpin Maintained Buy Rating and $1.25 Target
data.description:
Nicholas Cortellucci, an analyst from Atrium Research, maintained the Buy rating on Organto Foods. The associated price target remains the same with C$1.25.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Nicholas Cortellucci has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Organto’s accelerating growth and strengthening operating platform. He highlights that the company has reached roughly $2 million in weekly sales, implying an annualized revenue run rate near $100 million that is already closely aligned with his forward sales forecasts, and he expects Q4 results to showcase triple-digit year-over-year growth.

He also points to the deliberate expansion of Organto’s leadership bench and operational infrastructure, which he views as necessary to support sustained scale-up and upcoming profitability in 2026. In parallel, the company is broadening its retail presence into new European markets and bolstering its supply and logistics networks, reinforcing his conviction that the business is thriving and justifying the maintained Buy rating and $1.25 target price.

data.title: Medtronic: MiniMed IPO Dilution and Diabetes Spin-Off Limit Near-Term Upside, Justifying Hold Rating
data.description:
Medtronic, the Healthcare sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Rick Wise from Stifel Nicolaus maintained a Hold rating on the stock and has a $95.00 price target.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Rick Wise has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors related to Medtronic’s recent MiniMed IPO and its financial impact. He notes that, during the roughly six-month transition when MiniMed remains consolidated, Medtronic will face modest but ongoing EPS dilution from the 10% stake sold and an additional one-time charge tied to a MiniMed R&D agreement, which together reduce fiscal 2026 earnings guidance and temper near-term upside.

Looking out to fiscal 2027, Wise incorporates the full separation of the Diabetes unit, which removes its revenue but provides a modest gross margin lift as a lower-margin segment exits the portfolio. Even after adjusting for share retirement and the loss of Diabetes contributions, his updated model still points to only high‑single‑digit EPS growth, broadly aligned with management’s outlook. With his earnings forecasts cut and the price target reduced to $95 from $105, he concludes that the current risk‑reward profile is balanced, supporting a Hold rather than a more aggressive rating.

According to TipRanks, Wise is an analyst with an average return of -2.0% and a 45.00% success rate. Wise covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Boston Scientific, Edwards Lifesciences, and Outset Medical.

In another report released on April 1, Piper Sandler also reiterated a Hold rating on the stock with a $91.00 price target.

data.title: Balanced View on Neurocrine Biosciences: Favorable Soleno Acquisition Prospects Offset by Long-Term Ingrezza and Orphan Market Execution Risks
data.description:
Evan Seigerman, an analyst from BMO Capital, maintained the Hold rating on Neurocrine. The associated price target remains the same with $140.00.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Evan Seigerman has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to Neurocrine’s current and prospective portfolio. He views the potential Soleno acquisition favorably, noting that VYKAT XR offers a more prudent and strategically coherent entry into metabolic disease than earlier-stage GLP-1 pipeline ideas, and that Neurocrine’s strong cash position and free-cash-flow outlook provide ample capacity to pursue de-risked, commercial or near-commercial assets without stretching the balance sheet.

At the same time, his stance reflects caution that tempers enthusiasm about near-term opportunity with skepticism about longer-run upside. While he sees continued resilience from Ingrezza in the short term, he does not believe the franchise can fully meet consensus expectations over time, and he underscores that successful execution on the Soleno deal would still hinge on accelerating adoption and maximizing penetration in a relatively small, orphan population, leading him to maintain a Market Perform view on NBIX shares rather than move to a more bullish rating.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Balanced Risk/Reward Keeps Cal-Maine at Hold Amid Normalizing Earnings and Capped Pricing Upside
data.description:
Analyst Benjamin Mayhew from BMO Capital reiterated a Hold rating on Cal-Maine Foods and keeping the price target at $80.00.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Benjamin Mayhew has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to both near-term performance and longer-term industry dynamics. Cal-Maine’s recent quarter significantly exceeded expectations on the back of better conventional egg pricing and solid specialty volume growth, yet much of this strength reflects temporary pricing benefits and HPAI-driven retailer hedging that are unlikely to fully persist.

At the same time, new multi‑year pricing arrangements with retailers provide downside protection but cap upside, and overall earnings power is projected to normalize below $4 per share through fiscal 2027. While investments in specialty eggs, egg products, and prepared foods, along with a strong balance sheet and buyback capacity, support the story, oversupplied conditions and intensifying competition in U.S. eggs limit pricing power, leaving the risk/reward profile essentially balanced at the current valuation.

Mayhew covers the Consumer Defensive sector, focusing on stocks such as Cal-Maine Foods, The Andersons, and Vital Farms. According to TipRanks, Mayhew has an average return of -28.5% and a 28.57% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released on April 3, Goldman Sachs also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $82.00 price target.
